verdict_reasoning: TMED9 (transmembrane p24 trafficking protein 9) is an ER/Golgi cargo receptor and vesicle-trafficking protein. The p24 family proteins have a single-pass TM domain but reside in COPI/COPII vesicles and ER-Golgi membranes with their functional domain facing the endomembrane lumen, not the extracellular space. No surface display: the 'extracellular' face is the ER/Golgi lumen; the protein cycles within the early secretory pathway and does not reach the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets TMED9; (2) not an ecto-domain-shedding receptor; (3) not covalently tethered to a TM surface partner; (4) no documented PM minority pool — EV association (PMID 39766158) reflects EV cargo, not PM outer-leaflet display; (5) no cancer/cell-death/activation/germline-restricted outer-leaflet evidence. Detection in plasma EVs is consistent with endosomal/multivesicular-body routing, not bona-fide cell-surface exposure. Endomembrane-resident classification is appropriate.
